description: Add GitHub repository tooling: Dependabot for automated dependency updates, CodeQL for security scanning, branch protection configuration, PR auto-labeling by size and area, CODEOWNERS for auto-assigning reviewers, and a stale bot for closing inactive issues/PRs.

userStories.title: Add Dependabot configuration for automated dependency updates
userStories.description: Add a dependabot.yml config that monitors npm dependencies across the monorepo, docs, and plugins for updates and security vulnerabilities. Creates PRs automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at .github/dependabot.yml
Monitors npm ecosystem for the root monorepo (package.json)
Monitors npm ecosystem for docs/ (separate package.json)
Monitors github-actions ecosystem for workflow file updates
Update schedule is weekly
PRs are prefixed with 'deps:' for dependency updates
Groups minor and patch updates together to reduce PR noise
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .github/dependabot.yml (new file)

Context:
The repo has multiple package.json files:
- `/package.json` — root monorepo (npm workspaces: platform/*)
- `/docs/package.json` — standalone Next.js docs site
- `/plugins/*/package.json` — standalone plugins (NOT in workspace)

Plugins depend on published `@opentabs-dev/*` packages from npm, not local links. Dependabot should NOT monitor plugins — they get updated via the publish-plugins workflow. Only monitor root and docs.

Also monitor GitHub Actions versions (the `github-actions` ecosystem) to keep workflow actions up to date (we just had to bump @v4 to @v5 manually).

```yaml
version: 2
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: npm
    directory: /
    schedule:
      interval: weekly
    commit-message:
      prefix: "deps:"
    groups:
      minor-and-patch:
        update-types:
          - minor
          - patch
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10

  - package-ecosystem: npm
    directory: /docs
    schedule:
      interval: weekly
    commit-message:
      prefix: "deps(docs):"
    groups:
      minor-and-patch:
        update-types:
          - minor
          - patch
    open-pull-requests-limit: 5

  - package-ecosystem: github-actions
    directory: /
    schedule:
      interval: weekly
    commit-message:
      prefix: "ci:"
    open-pull-requests-limit: 5
```

The `groups` feature batches minor+patch updates into a single PR instead of one PR per dependency. Major updates still get individual PRs since they may have breaking changes.

Constraints:
- Do NOT add plugin directories — they are updated separately
- Keep open-pull-requests-limit reasonable to avoid PR flood

userStories.title: Add CodeQL security scanning workflow
userStories.description: Add a CodeQL analysis workflow that scans TypeScript/JavaScript code for security vulnerabilities on every push and PR.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow file exists at .github/workflows/codeql.yml
Scans JavaScript/TypeScript code
Runs on push to main and on pull_request
Uses actions/checkout@v5 and github/codeql-action@v3
Results appear in the GitHub Security tab
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .github/workflows/codeql.yml (new file)

Context:
CodeQL is GitHub's semantic code analysis engine. It's free for public repos and integrates with the Security tab. It detects vulnerabilities like:
- Command injection
- Path traversal
- Prototype pollution
- Regex DoS
- XSS
- Unsafe deserialization

```yaml
name: CodeQL

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * 1'  # Weekly Monday 6am UTC

concurrency:
  group: codeql-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  analyze:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      security-events: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Initialize CodeQL
        uses: github/codeql-action/init@v3
        with:
          languages: javascript-typescript

      - name: Perform CodeQL Analysis
        uses: github/codeql-action/analyze@v3
```

The `schedule` trigger runs weekly to catch new vulnerability patterns even when no code changes. The `security-events: write` permission is required for CodeQL to post results to the Security tab.

Constraints:
- Use `javascript-typescript` language (covers both JS and TS)
- Use github/codeql-action@v3 (latest stable)
- Use actions/checkout@v5 (not v4, to avoid Node.js 20 deprecation warning)

userStories.title: Add PR auto-labeling by size and area
userStories.description: Add a workflow that automatically labels PRs by size (XS/S/M/L/XL based on diff lines) and by area (platform, plugins, docs, e2e, ci) based on changed file paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow file exists at .github/workflows/pr-labeler.yml
Triggers on pull_request opened and synchronize events
Labels PRs with size: XS (<10 lines), S (<50), M (<200), L (<500), XL (500+)
Labels PRs with area based on paths: platform, plugins, docs, e2e, ci
A PR can have multiple area labels
Label config file exists at .github/labeler.yml for path-based labels
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .github/workflows/pr-labeler.yml (new file)
- .github/labeler.yml (new file — path-to-label mapping)

Context:
Two labeling mechanisms:

1. **Size labels** — based on total lines changed. Use a simple script (no external action needed):
```yaml
- name: Label PR size
  uses: actions/github-script@v7
  with:
    script: |
      const { data: pr } = await github.rest.pulls.get({
        owner: context.repo.owner,
        repo: context.repo.repo,
        pull_number: context.issue.number,
      });
      const lines = pr.additions + pr.deletions;
      const sizeLabels = { 'size: XS': 10, 'size: S': 50, 'size: M': 200, 'size: L': 500 };
      let label = 'size: XL';
      for (const [name, max] of Object.entries(sizeLabels)) {
        if (lines < max) { label = name; break; }
      }
      // Remove existing size labels
      const existing = (await github.rest.issues.listLabelsOnIssue({
        owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo, issue_number: context.issue.number
      })).data.map(l => l.name).filter(n => n.startsWith('size:'));
      for (const old of existing) {
        if (old !== label) await github.rest.issues.removeLabel({ owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo, issue_number: context.issue.number, name: old }).catch(() => {});
      }
      await github.rest.issues.addLabels({ owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo, issue_number: context.issue.number, labels: [label] });
```

2. **Area labels** — use `actions/labeler@v5` with a config file:

`.github/labeler.yml`:
```yaml
platform:
  - changed-files:
    - any-glob-to-any-file: 'platform/**'

plugins:
  - changed-files:
    - any-glob-to-any-file: 'plugins/**'

docs:
  - changed-files:
    - any-glob-to-any-file: 'docs/**'

e2e:
  - changed-files:
    - any-glob-to-any-file: 'e2e/**'

ci:
  - changed-files:
    - any-glob-to-any-file: '.github/**'
```

The workflow combines both:
```yaml
name: PR Labels

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  label:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/labeler@v5
        with:
          repo-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - name: Label PR size
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            // ... size labeling script above
```

Constraints:
- Labels must be created automatically (the actions create them if they don't exist)
- Size labels are mutually exclusive (only one at a time)
- Area labels are NOT mutually exclusive (a PR can touch platform + e2e)

userStories.title: Add CODEOWNERS file for auto-assigning PR reviewers
userStories.description: Add a CODEOWNERS file that maps code areas to responsible reviewers so PRs automatically request reviews from the right people.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at .github/CODEOWNERS
Maps platform/ to the core team
Maps plugins/ to the plugin team or core team
Maps docs/ to the docs team or core team
Maps .github/ to the core team
Maps e2e/ to the core team
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .github/CODEOWNERS (new file)

Context:
CODEOWNERS maps file paths to GitHub users or teams. When a PR modifies files matching a pattern, those owners are automatically requested as reviewers.

Since this is currently a small team, use the GitHub org team or individual handles. Check the repo's contributors/org for the right handles.

For a small project, a simple CODEOWNERS is better than an overly granular one:

```
# Default — core team reviews everything
* @opentabs-dev/core

# Docs — docs team or anyone familiar with the docs site
/docs/ @opentabs-dev/core

# Plugins — plugin maintainers
/plugins/ @opentabs-dev/core

# CI/CD — infrastructure
/.github/ @opentabs-dev/core
```

If the `@opentabs-dev/core` team doesn't exist yet, use individual GitHub usernames instead. Check the repo owner's username.

Actually, for a solo/small project, the simplest CODEOWNERS is:
```
* @xzhangopentabs
```

Or whatever the repo owner's GitHub handle is. Check `git log --format='%an <%ae>' | sort -u | head -5` for contributor handles.

Constraints:
- Keep it simple — don't over-specify for a small team
- Use the actual GitHub username(s) of the project maintainer(s)
- The file must be at .github/CODEOWNERS (not root CODEOWNERS)

userStories.title: Add stale bot for closing inactive issues and PRs
userStories.description: Add a workflow that automatically marks issues and PRs as stale after 60 days of inactivity and closes them after 14 more days.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow file exists at .github/workflows/stale.yml
Runs on a weekly schedule
Marks issues as stale after 60 days of inactivity
Closes stale issues after 14 more days with no activity
Marks PRs as stale after 30 days of inactivity
Closes stale PRs after 7 more days
Issues/PRs with 'pinned' or 'security' labels are exempt
Bot leaves a clear comment explaining why it's being closed
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .github/workflows/stale.yml (new file)

Context:
Use the official `actions/stale@v9` action.

```yaml
name: Stale Issues and PRs

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 8 * * 1'  # Weekly Monday 8am UTC
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  issues: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  stale:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/stale@v9
        with:
          stale-issue-message: >
            This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has not had
            any activity in 60 days. It will be closed in 14 days if no further activity
            occurs. If this issue is still relevant, please leave a comment.
          stale-pr-message: >
            This pull request has been automatically marked as stale because it has not had
            any activity in 30 days. It will be closed in 7 days if no further activity
            occurs. If this PR is still in progress, please leave a comment or push a commit.
          close-issue-message: >
            This issue was closed automatically because it was stale for too long.
            Feel free to reopen if the issue is still relevant.
          close-pr-message: >
            This pull request was closed automatically because it was stale for too long.
            Feel free to reopen if this work is still needed.
          days-before-stale: 60
          days-before-close: 14
          days-before-pr-stale: 30
          days-before-pr-close: 7
          stale-issue-label: stale
          stale-pr-label: stale
          exempt-issue-labels: pinned,security,bug
          exempt-pr-labels: pinned,security
          operations-per-run: 100
```

Constraints:
- Exempt labels prevent important issues from being auto-closed
- `operations-per-run: 100` prevents hitting GitHub API rate limits
- The `workflow_dispatch` trigger allows manual runs for testing
- Use clear, friendly messages — this is an open source project

userStories.title: Add deploy preview for docs PRs
userStories.description: Add a workflow that deploys a preview of the docs site to Cloudflare Pages for every PR that modifies docs/, with the preview URL posted as a PR comment.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow triggers on pull_request when docs/ files change
Builds the docs site from the PR branch
Deploys to a Cloudflare Pages preview URL (branch deploy)
Posts a comment on the PR with the preview URL
Preview URL is unique per PR
